channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Regulators order corrective action as Keystone Pipeline operators aim to restore service
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — Federal regulators have ordered the operator of the Keystone Pipeline to take several corrective actions after a rupture caused 147,000 gallons of oil to spill onto farmland in North Dakota, and the company said its goal is to resume deliveries to refineries on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration says it will exclude some electronics from reciprocal tariffs
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — The Trump administration late Friday said they would exclude electronics like smartphones and laptops from reciprocal tariffs, a move that could help keep the prices down for popular consumer electronics that aren’t usually made in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MAE ANDERSON and MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Kansas man who was falsely accused of being a shooter after Chiefs’ 2024 Super Bowl win has died
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — A Kansas man who was falsely accused of being among the shooters who opened fire at a rally celebrating the Kansas City Chiefs’ Super Bowl victory last year has died, his attorney said. Denton Loudermill Jr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Popular soup, bowl products may be contaminated; USDA issues public health alert
item.description: The USDA has issued a nationwide public health alert warning consumers that some popular soup and bowl products could be contaminated with wood.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KREX 5

item.title: Pet food recalled after testing positive for salmonella and listeria: FDA
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has announced a voluntary recall of certain puppy and kitten foods.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Texas man suing NFL player over December shooting at car lot in Nashville
item.description: A Texas man who says Titans cornerback L'Jarius Sneed or his personal assistant shot at him while he was sitting inside a Mercedes-Benz on a dealer lot in December is suing for at least $1 million.
item.dc:creator: Teresa M. Walker, KRON4

item.title: 5 major unreleased movies we'll (probably) never get to see
item.description: "Coyote vs. Acme" was recently rescued from obscurity after being shelved by Warner Bros. Discovery. But other films haven't been so lucky.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Trans 'Zizian' cult suspect dragged from court after shouting allegations of de-transition, murder: report
item.description: A member of a radical transgender vegan cult suspected in the attempted murder of a California landlord claimed in court that guards tried to de-transition her.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump exempts phones, computers, other electronics from reciprocal tariffs
item.description: The Trump administration is exempting electronics such as phones, computers and other electronics from the "reciprocal" tariffs imposed on other nations, including China.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An emboldened anti-abortion faction wants women who have abortions to face criminal charges
item.description: Many people involved in the abortion debate say a movement of so-called abortion abolitionists who want to punish women for having abortions is widening its influence.
item.dc:creator: Christine Fernando, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What we know about the New York City helicopter crash that killed 6 people
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New York City sightseeing helicopter broke apart and crashed into the Hudson River near the New Jersey shoreline, killing the pilot and a Spanish family of five who were on board.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Utahns express concerns over potential impacts of 'SAVE Act' on women voters
item.description: A bill under consideration in Washington could change the way you register to vote, and critics believe it could disenfranchise women at the polls.
item.dc:creator: McKenzie Diaz, ABC 4

item.title: How a fight over proxy voting for new parents upended the US House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Anna Paulina Luna was at her Florida home in fall 2023, caring for her newborn son and turning over a question in her mind as a member of Congress: “How do I change this?” Luna, then a first-time mom and first-term lawmaker, could no longer fly to Washington to cast votes in
item.dc:creator: LEAH ASKARINAM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Woman unknowingly gives birth to a stranger's baby after IVF clinic error
item.description: A woman in Australia unknowingly gave birth to a stranger’s baby after she received another patient's embryo from her in vitro fertilization clinic due to “human error,” the clinic said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump admin adds migrant Social Security numbers to 'death list' in deportation push
item.description: The request from Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem to place the migrants on the “Ineligible Master File” maintained by the SSA would bar migrants from receiving benefits or being able to work in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Judge rules Menendez brothers’ bid for freedom through resentencing can continue
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Erik and Lyle Menendez’s resentencing hearings can continue despite opposition from the Los Angeles County district attorney, a judge ruled Friday.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil can be deported, immigration judge rules
item.description: JENA, La. (AP) — Columbia University graduate student Mahmoud Khalil can be forced out of the country as a national security risk, an immigration judge in Louisiana ruled Friday after lawyers argued the legality of deporting the activist who participated in pro-Palestinian demonstrations.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: South Carolina executes second man by firing squad in 5 weeks
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A firing squad on Friday executed a South Carolina man who killed an off-duty police officer, the second time the rare execution method has been used by the state in the past five weeks.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: South Carolina executes second man by firing squad in 5 weeks
item.description: A firing squad on Friday executed a South Carolina man who killed an off-duty police officer, the second time the rare execution method has been used by the state in the past five weeks.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Collins, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US may already be in recession: BlackRock CEO
item.description: BlackRock CEO Larry Fink said during a recent interview that the United States may already be in a recession or getting very close to one as a result of President Trump’s sweeping tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump eyes major cuts to NOAA research
item.description: The Trump administration is eyeing cuts to climate, weather and ocean research in a draft budget blueprint for the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Fox News True Crime Newsletter: Kohberger's evidence fight, Vallow's latest trial, Read's jury selection
item.description: Stay up to date with the Fox News True Crime Newsletter, which brings you the latest cases ripped from the headlines, from crime to courts, legal and scandal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration adds immigrant Social Security numbers to 'death list' in deportation push
item.description: The Trump administration directed the Social Security Administration to place more than 6,300 migrants on its list for tracking dead people, blocking their ability to work in a move it hopes will encourage them to “self-deport.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Schools lined up for help getting cleaner school buses. Then came the EPA freeze
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Looking to cut pollution from its fleet of diesel-burning school buses, an Oklahoma school district last year doubled its electric vehicles.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and ALEXA ST. JOHN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Social Security Administration 'will be using X to communicate' moving forward
item.description: The shift comes as communications staff at the agency has dwindled due to reassignments in front-facing roles at field offices across the country.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Black Power Ranger actor disagrees with writer on his casting
item.description: Walter Emanual Jones, the actor who portrayed the Black Power Ranger in the "Mighty Morphin Power Rangers," responded to a statement made by one of the series' writers calling the casting of a Black actor in the role a "mistake.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5

item.title: Amazon CEO: Sellers will try to pass tariff costs along to buyers
item.description: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy predicted that the retailer’s web of third-party sellers will try to pass the extra costs incurred by the Trump administration's wide-ranging tariffs on to buyers.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Here's a look at recent aircraft tragedies, mishaps and close calls
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Six people were killed Thursday in the latest string of aviation disasters after a sightseeing helicopter broke apart and crashed into the Hudson River between New York City and New Jersey. The crash comes at a time of heightened scrutiny of aircraft collisions and near-misses.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NYC university sued, accused of not taking action against antisemitic harassment
item.description: Brandon White is suing the New School after the university allegedly failed to take action on harassment. White, who is not Jewish, claims to have been targeted by pro-Palestinian agitators.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Grand jury indicts man for threats against Augusta National employee
item.description: A Georgia grand jury has returned an indictment against a man for his role in making threats against an employee of the Augusta National Golf Club.
item.dc:creator: D.V. Wise, Fox 5

item.title: Homeland Security agents denied entry at two Los Angeles schools
item.description: It is believed to be the first time federal agents tried to enter Los Angeles schools since President Trump announced a crackdown on immigration.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5

item.title: Jury awards $1.68 billion to women who accused writer and director James Toback of sexual abuse
item.description: The judgement marks one of the largest jury awards since the advent of the #MeToo movement, as well as in New York state history
item.dc:creator: Susan Haigh, KRON4

item.title: Sweepstakes giant Publishers Clearing House files for bankruptcy
item.description: Publishers Clearing House, famous for its big checks and sweepstakes, files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy as it shifts to digital advertising.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, Fox 5

item.title: Hope of finding survivors fades in aftermath of Dominican club roof collapse
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — Rescue crews in the Dominican Republic on Thursday dug through the remains of a legendary nightclub whose roof collapsed earlier this week, killing at least 184 people, but hope of finding survivors was slim.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA and DÁNICA COTO Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sam's Club plans to open 15 new locations a year, CEO says
item.description: Sam's Club CEO Chris Nicholas says the company plans to open 15 new locations a year "for the foreseeable future.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, Fox 5

item.title: 1,700 pounds of butter recalled over bacteria contamination
item.description: Nearly a ton of salted butter is being recalled because, according to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration, the product is "contaminated with elevated levels of coliform," a bacteria found in animals' digestive tracts and waste.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Human remains found in 1973 identified as missing Kansas teen
item.description: Human remains found near Garnett, Kansas, in 1973 have been positively identified more than 50 years later.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, Fox 5
